bhadrapads pulses and those who are in charge of rivers are harmed.
Towards the end of the chapter he changes the metre and
the following three verses are in three different metres. In the
next verse the author discusses the effects of the mutual positions
of Jupiter and Saturn.]

When Jupiter tenants the star Visakha and Saturn Krttika,
there will be dreadful unrest and violence among the subjects; and
when these two planets occupy one and the same constellation, the
capital will be stormed.

Similarly the conjunction of Saturn and Jupiter (which is called
in-astrology) in the dual Rasis is considered harmful.

If Saturn should appear with many colours, he would destroy
birds (or fishes); if he be possessed of yellowish rays, he would
cause the threat of famine (or famine and fear); if he be blood-red,
